title: About Pylons
wikitext:
[]: About Pylons             Quote Post by visionfactory » Fri Nov 12, 2010 9:34 pm Can somebody explain me why Adam has pylons? Aren't pylons created by humans? I thought that pylons were used as a battery source as seen in the picture And they serve to save inside the progressive knife and attack as seen in EoE And why Eva 00 (yellow) doesn't have pylons? What he can't do that the other Evas (units 00 blue, 01, 02, 03, 04) can? MP Evas don't have pylons, I think it's because the internal battery of 5 minutes is inside the pylons and they don't need it anymore as they have a N2 reactor inside, am I right? So if Adam has pylons it means that he has a internal battery and progressive knife inside (?) I don't think he is supposed to have these but since he has pylons...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Nov 12, 2010 9:49 pm The pylons aren't used as batteries for internal energy. In episode 13, the Evas have those battery packs because they had no other source of energy in the blackout. As for Adam, those pylons are there because the Katsuragi team had put restraints on it, which is really all the pylons are used for: restraint.

[]: Re: About Pylons             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Nov 12, 2010 9:50 pm visionfactory wrote: Can somebody explain me why Adam has pylons? Aren't pylons created by humans?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Nov 12, 2010 9:56 pm Sachi wrote: As for Adam, those pylons are there because the Katsuragi team had put restraints on it, which is really all the pylons are used for: restraint.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Nov 12, 2010 10:03 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Of course they're some kind of fake restraint placed more for show than for their usefulness.

[]: Re: About Pylons             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Nov 12, 2010 10:21 pm Azathoth wrote: The pylons don't contain the battery - in fact, I don't think there's any indication of where exactly the battery is located. Their primary function is restraint.

[]: Re: About Pylons             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Nov 12, 2010 10:30 pm NemZ wrote: watch ep11 again etc.

[]: Re: About Pylons             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Fri Nov 12, 2010 11:01 pm Azathoth wrote: In its original configuration, Unit 00 was unreliable and near-useless (being a cobbled-together prototype). After being reworked, pylons were added, and Unit 00 stopped sucking so much.

[]: Re: About Pylons             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Nov 12, 2010 11:11 pm Sun Stealer wrote: So what you are saying is that Unit 00 required additional pylons? I am sorry, but it had to be said.

[]: Re: About Pylons             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Nov 12, 2010 11:49 pm @Azathoth: I think I misunderstood you. If you were referring to the internal batteries then no, there's no indication at all where they are located.

[]: Re: About Pylons             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Nov 13, 2010 12:01 am Sun Stealer wrote: So what you are saying is that Unit 00 required additional pylons?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Nov 13, 2010 2:03 am I would assume that the internal batteries are located somewhere along the back region, or possible within the torso, since the umbilical cables plug into the back.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Sat Nov 13, 2010 6:37 am because the viewer needs to imediately say "god its eva". And by the way eva's are female.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Nov 13, 2010 12:11 pm TheLobe wrote: And by the way eva's have no sex .

[]:             Quote Post by TonyTH » Sat Nov 13, 2010 8:04 pm I thought armor as a whole were the restraints. I figured the pylons were honestly JUST for sending them up the launch elevators.

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Sat Nov 13, 2010 10:38 pm Don't forget the cool-ass spike launchers on Unit 02 and the iconic Progressive Knives. You can stick anything ranging from over-sized Slash Harkens to CIWS guns to plasma TVs in there

[]:             Quote Post by lilim » Thu Nov 18, 2010 12:02 am for some reason i think Asuka would be distracted if there were TVs in her Unit 02. but maybe i'm just not giving her enough credit. >>;

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Thu Nov 18, 2010 6:39 am lol she could have watched FIFA Germany vs Spain and flown into a berkerk rage and own'd Zeurel, who might also be engrossed in the game. After all, sports transcend both race and gender, right?

[]:             Quote Post by lilim » Thu Nov 18, 2010 9:43 am haha, i come from Alabama where half the fanatic football fans i know are women, so definitely gender doesn't matter. besides, "rabid sports fan" totally fits Asuka's personality! i wanna see fan art of this now.. and it'd be interesting to see the angel watching TV, for sure xD

[]:             Quote Post by Nightweaver20xx » Sun Nov 21, 2010 1:10 am Well, you asked for it...
